mission.title: keycloak: Get multiple users by Ids
mission.description: Get multiple users by Ids. Requested by 31+ users.

steps.title: Check current keycloak setup
steps.description:
Verify your keycloak version and configuration:
```bash
kc.sh --version 2>/dev/null || bin/kc.sh --version
```
This feature requires a working keycloak installation.

steps.title: Review keycloak configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant keycloak configuration:
There is currently a get user by id and a get users with query string and search filtering. Can we get an id field added to the search for users API that takes in an array of user ids?

steps.title: Apply the fix for Get multiple users by Ids
steps.description:
My use case was a "Member" relation similar to private GitHub Repositories where you can invite Users as "Members" (or other roles, a.k.a. Collaborators).

In my case a Member is a relationship object for a many-to-many relation between (something like) Repositories and Users.

```ts
interface Member {
  projectId: string; // whatever
  userId: string; // keycloak uuid
}
```

My backend would provide a list of members given a project id:

```http
GET

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Get multiple users by Ids" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
My use case was a "Member" relation similar to private GitHub Repositories where you can invite Users as "Members" (or other roles, a.k.a. Collaborators).

In my case a Member is a relationship object for a many-to-many relation between (something like) Repositories and Users.

prerequisites.description: A working keycloak installation or development environment.
